title: Attributes, Filters, Merge Drivers, and Content Policy · Repository Repair Bay
summary: A responder inherits a plausible but defective path-attribute and content-transformation policy; preserve reachable history, isolate the first causal Git-state mismatch, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in path-attribute and content-transformation policy and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence.
Design text, eol, working-tree-encoding, binary, and renormalization policy with byte-level and cross-platform verification.
Configure diff drivers, textconv, binary markers, merge drivers, recursive behavior, and conflict-marker size without treating presentation as storage.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Case G13 is a debug in a disposable repository. a directory-level attribute overrides expected line-ending and filter policy. The learner must predict the effective text, eol, filter, diff, and merge states are reported for the exact path, run git check-attr --all -- scripts/release.sh, explain release.sh is text with eol=crlf because a nearer attribute rule overrides root policy, test this changed condition: remove the required filter tool and require checkout to fail instead of silently losing data, and preserve this recovery boundary: record byte digests before renormalization and retain the pre-migration ref. Competency probe: Resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence.

Inspect the Gita Attribute Precedence Query trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence.

content.text: For Gita Attribute Precedence Query, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: Name the Gita Attribute Precedence Query precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G13 is a debug in a disposable repository. a directory-level attribute overrides expected line-ending and filter policy. The learner must predict the effective text, eol, filter, diff, and merge states are reported for the exact path, run git check-attr --all -- scripts/release.sh, explain release.sh is text with eol=crlf because a nearer attribute rule overrides root policy, test this changed condition: remove the required filter tool and require checkout to fail instead of silently losing data, and preserve this recovery boundary: record byte digests before renormalization and retain the pre-migration ref. Competency probe: Resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Attributes use the same pattern semantics and precedence as .gitignore.

stimulus.title: Gita Attribute Precedence Query case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Case G13 is a debug in a disposable repository. a directory-level attribute overrides expected line-ending and filter policy. The learner must predict the effective text, eol, filter, diff, and merge states are reported for the exact path, run git check-attr --all -- scripts/release.sh, explain release.sh is text with eol=crlf because a nearer attribute rule overrides root policy, test this changed condition: remove the required filter tool and require checkout to fail instead of silently losing data, and preserve this recovery boundary: record byte digests before renormalization and retain the pre-migration ref. lines.label: risk
lines.text: Attributes use the same pattern semantics and precedence as .gitignore.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Resolve attributes from system, repository, info, directory, pattern, and negative states using check-attr evidence. Artifact under review: path-attribute and content-transformation policy.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Gita Attribute Precedence Query, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Gita Attribute Precedence Query record says path-attribute and content-transformation policy looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. This does not resolve inspect the failure record.

For inspect the failure record, apply Gita Attribute Precedence Query without its stated preconditions, omit the path-attribute and content-transformation policy trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Gita Attribute Precedence Query for path-attribute and content-transformation policy.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-large-content-policy-177s5z" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Large Content Policy into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-large-content-policy-177s5z
# scenario-key: trace-177s5z probe-z5s771 boundary-7s5z17 owner-177s5zx fault-177s5zz
# before-ref: main=a11ce17 candidate=b00b15z
# hypothesis: a directory-level attribute overrides expected line-ending and filter policy
git check-attr --all -- scripts/release.sh
# expected: the effective text, eol, filter, diff, and merge states are reported for the exact path
# observation: release.sh is text with eol=crlf because a nearer attribute rule overrides root policy
# changed-case: remove the required filter tool and require checkout to fail instead of silently losing data
# after-ref: main=a11ce17 candidate=c0ffeez
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: record byte digests before renormalization and retain the pre-migration ref
# transfer-limit: test real filters and encodings across supported platforms in disposable clones
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Gita Large Content Policy structural check is only one gate. path-attribute and content-transformation policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.

hints: Name the Gita Large Content Policy precondition or invariant that the debug case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Case G14 is a debug in a disposable repository. a directory-level attribute overrides expected line-ending and filter policy. Competency probe: Choose ordinary Git, LFS-style pointers, release artifacts, package registries, or external storage from review, locking, retention, availability, and migration needs.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Any large file automatically belongs in a pointer-based extension regardless of access and history needs.
